description: A two-lane frontage road flanked by grassy thickets. The concrete bulkheads of Interstate 71 curtain off the wilderness to the north.
dayDesc: A two-lane frontage road flanked by grassy thickets. The concrete bulkheads of Interstate 71 curtain off the wilderness to the north.
nightDesc: A two-lane frontage road flanked by croaking, chittering thickets. The highway's silhouette merges with the black sky.

exits.description: To the west, the highway exit descends.
A farm road branches off the frontage to the south.

description: Interstate 71 exits here at a nine-way stoplight with two dedicated truck lanes.
dayDesc: Interstate 71 exits here at a nine-way stoplight with two dedicated truck lanes.
nightDesc: Interstate 71 exits here at a nine-way stoplight with two dedicated truck lanes. The lights semaphore pointlessly in the dark.

exits.description: To the east, the frontage runs to a private road into some woodland.
To the south, an intersection with shop signs.

description: A wide concrete intersection hosts a cavernous, hula-hutted Dairy Queen, an Economy Island extended-stay and a gas station.
dayDesc: A wide concrete intersection hosts a cavernous, hula-hutted Dairy Queen, an Economy Island extended-stay and a gas station.
nightDesc: A wide concrete intersection hosts a sleeping restaurant, a dreary-looking motel and a gas station: brightly-lit, the contents of its glass on display.

exits.description: An intimidating vehicle mixup flashes signals to the north.
Locke continues south to the crossroads of the town of Lakeland.
On Gardner to the west are some houses whose modesty says pre-war.
The Dairy Queen's glass doors face the intersection.
The gas station's forest-green bear sign glows over the pumps.

description: A gas station convenience store under humming fluorescents. The forest-green bear mascot grins from every surface — cups, bags, the clock above the register. The shelves are surprisingly well-stocked.
dayDesc: A gas station convenience store. Sunlight through the glass front makes the linoleum shine. The coffee is fresh. The bear grins.
nightDesc: A gas station convenience store, the brightest thing for a mile. Moths ping against the windows. The coffee has been sitting since morning. The bear grins.

exits.description: The glass doors open to the intersection and the pumps.

description: A dirt road cuts between fallow soybean fields. Tire ruts full of standing water reflect the sky. A rusted grain elevator stands in the middle distance like a monument.
dayDesc: A dirt road cuts between fallow soybean fields. Tire ruts full of standing water reflect the sky. A rusted grain elevator stands in the middle distance like a monument.
nightDesc: The fields are a flat darkness broken only by the faint gleam of water in the tire ruts. Something large moves in the soybeans.

exits.description: The frontage road runs north along the highway.
The dirt road continues south past a collapsed fence.

description: The road dwindles to two bare tracks through waist-high grass. A caved-in barn sags to the east. The air smells of wet earth and something animal.
dayDesc: The road dwindles to two bare tracks through waist-high grass. A caved-in barn sags to the east. Crows wheel overhead in a slow gyre.
nightDesc: The road dwindles to two bare tracks through waist-high grass. The barn is a black shape against a marginally less black sky. Eyes reflect from the grass.

exits.description: The dirt road leads north through the fields.

description: A cul-de-sac of small clapboard houses with chain-link yards. A basketball hoop with no net leans over a cracked driveway. Most of the mailboxes are empty.
dayDesc: A cul-de-sac of small clapboard houses with chain-link yards. A dog barks somewhere. Dandelions have colonized every lawn.
nightDesc: A cul-de-sac of small clapboard houses. A single porch light is on. The rest of the houses are dark, but a television flickers blue behind curtains.

exits.description: Gardner runs east to the intersection at I-71.

description: The crossroads of Lakeland: a stoplight, a post office, and a dollar store. Locke Street runs north-south. A side street heads east toward what passes for downtown.
dayDesc: The crossroads of Lakeland. The dollar store is open. An elderly man sits on a bench outside the post office reading a newspaper from last week.
nightDesc: The crossroads of Lakeland. The stoplight blinks yellow. The dollar store's sign buzzes, half its letters dark.

exits.description: Locke runs north toward the highway interchange.
A side street leads east into a block of old storefronts.
Locke continues south past the post office.

description: A half-block of pre-war storefronts: a barbershop, a thrift store, a place that sells bait. The sidewalk is buckled where tree roots have won their slow war with concrete.
dayDesc: A half-block of pre-war storefronts. The barbershop pole still turns. An orange cat sleeps in the thrift store window.
nightDesc: A half-block of pre-war storefronts, all closed. The barbershop pole is still. Between two buildings, an alley leads to a warehouse whose lights are on.

exits.description: The side street runs west to the crossroads.
A narrow alley cuts between the barbershop and the thrift store.

description: The south end of Locke Street. The houses thin out and give way to light industrial lots behind chain-link. A logistics company has a fenced yard to the east.
dayDesc: The south end of Locke Street. A truck idles at the logistics yard gate. Heat shimmers off the asphalt.
nightDesc: The south end of Locke Street. Sodium lights buzz over the logistics yard. The residential windows are all dark.

exits.description: The crossroads are to the north.
A gate in the chain-link opens to the logistics yard.

description: A gleaming corporate ice cream retailer. There's no one at the counter. The menu board flickers between seasonal specials for two different seasons.

exits.description: There's a bathroom next to the trash and the ketchup.
Outside the exit is a narrow parking lot and a wide intersection.

description: A gleaming corporate bathroom. It smells like antiseptic. The fluorescent light hums at a frequency that makes your fillings ache.

exits.description: A seam by the toilet leaves this world.
exits.blockMessage: There's a hairline crack in the tile by the toilet. It seems like it could be wider than it is.

exits.description: The swinging doors exit to the restaurant.

description: A gravel yard behind chain-link topped with razor wire. Loading pallets are stacked in rows. A forklift sits abandoned mid-turn, keys still in the ignition.
dayDesc: A gravel yard baking in the sun. The razor wire throws sharp shadows. A crow sits on the forklift.
nightDesc: A gravel yard under sodium floods. The shadows are hard-edged and orange. The forklift looks like it's been sitting there for years.

exits.description: The gate opens to Locke Street.
The warehouse's loading doors are half-open.

description: A vast empty warehouse with some suspicious-looking grease stains. A floodlight tries to light it all and cannot.

exits.description: A small, dimly-lit box stands apart in the corner.
Double-doors lead to the yard.

description: A plywood office with a metal desk, a calendar three years out of date, and a coffee mug growing something green. The computer monitor is smashed. A window looks out into the warehouse darkness.

exits.description: The office door opens to the warehouse floor.

description: The interior of a warehouse has been converted into a bar, but it's so dimly lit you'd never find it. Booming trap from a jukebox makes it impossible to hear.

exits.description: The alley leads back to the storefronts on Main Street.

description: A strip of dark volcanic sand between the water and a low seawall. Fishing boats are pulled up in a row, their hulls crusted with salt. The hotel tower rises to the south.
dayDesc: A strip of dark volcanic sand. The water is impossibly blue. Fishing nets dry on stakes driven into the sand.
nightDesc: A strip of dark sand under stars. The fishing boats creak against each other. Lights from the hotel reflect in the wet sand.

exits.description: A concrete pier extends north along the waterline.
A paved road curves south to a hotel entrance.

description: A freshly-paved street loops before the hotel, providing taxis a place to drop passengers without stopping.
dayDesc: A freshly-paved street loops before the hotel, providing taxis a place to drop passengers without stopping.
nightDesc: A freshly-paved street loops before the hotel, providing taxis a place to drop passengers without stopping. Sea breeze whips in from the shore to the north.

exits.description: A hotel tower stands to the south.
The shore road curves north to the waterfront.

description: The Hotel Saint-Saens is woody, carpeted and chrome. It smells like a half-frozen air conditioner.

exits.description: The front doors open to the outdoor forecourt.
A chrome double-door leads to the bar.
The elevators skulk in the dark to the southeast.

description: A long mahogany bar under copper pendant lights. The shelves behind are fully stocked but there's no bartender. A jazz record plays on a turntable behind the bar, needle riding the runout groove.
dayDesc: A long mahogany bar. Daylight filters through plantation shutters, striping the copper fixtures. The turntable is off.
nightDesc: A long mahogany bar under warm copper light. The bottles glow amber. A jazz record has been playing the same runout groove for hours.

exits.description: Chrome double-doors lead back to the lobby.

description: A dim alcove with two brass elevator doors, tarnished green at the edges. One elevator's indicator is stuck between floors. The other dings softly, doors open, empty.

exits.description: The lobby opens up to the northwest.
The working elevator waits, doors open.

description: A carpeted hallway on an upper floor. The room numbers skip: 3, 5, 8, 13. At the far end, a window looks out on a strip of gleaming apartment complexes on a hillside.
dayDesc: A carpeted hallway on an upper floor. The room numbers skip: 3, 5, 8, 13. At the far end, a window looks out on a strip of gleaming condos on a hillside.
nightDesc: A carpeted hallway on an upper floor. The room numbers skip: 3, 5, 8, 13. At the far end, a window looks out on a mosaic of glowing lights from the buildings a hillside.

exits.description: The elevator alcove is here.

description: A collection of cement houses painted blue and salmon-pink cluster around the meeting of two semi-paved roads. The houses have strips of white ceramic tile with a green diamond pattern.
dayDesc: A collection of simple, cement houses cluster around the meeting of two semi-paved roads. Motorbikes drone somewhere nearby.
nightDesc: A collection of simple, cement houses cluster around the meeting of two semi-paved roads. Cold glare from an overhead halogen lights the intersection.

exits.description: The road south disappears into dense jungle.
To the west a road cuts between forest and field.
To the east the road stops at a fishing pier.

description: A concrete pier extending over shallow turquoise water. Fishing boats are tied along one side. Nets hang drying from crooked wooden posts.
dayDesc: A concrete pier over shallow turquoise water. Children dive off the end. The catch of the day lies silver and gasping in plastic bins.
nightDesc: A concrete pier over black water. The boats knock against the pilings. A single lantern hangs from a post, swinging in the breeze.

exits.description: The village road leads west.
A path follows the waterline south toward the hotel shore.

description: Dense tropical scrub presses in from both sides of a deteriorating road. Machete marks on the branches are old. Birds you can't name shriek from the canopy.
dayDesc: Dense tropical scrub. The road is barely visible under encroaching vegetation. Butterflies the size of your hand drift between shafts of light.
nightDesc: The scrub is a wall of black on both sides. Things rustle and snap in the undergrowth. The road is felt more than seen.

exits.description: The road leads east to the village.
A footpath breaks south through the undergrowth.

description: A natural clearing in the scrub where volcanic rock breaks through the soil. Feral goat droppings are everywhere. A collapsed stone wall suggests someone farmed here once.
dayDesc: A natural clearing. The volcanic rock is warm to the touch. Lizards sun themselves on the old wall. The jungle buzzes.
nightDesc: A clearing under open sky. The stars are violent. Something has been digging near the collapsed wall.

exits.description: The footpath leads north into the thicket.
A game trail winds east into the jungle.

description: A trail cuts through dense jungle. Roots cross the path like veins. The canopy is so thick the light is green and submarine.
dayDesc: A trail through dense jungle. The light is green and submarine. Water drips from leaves that haven't seen direct sun in decades.
nightDesc: A trail through dense jungle. Total darkness. The canopy blocks even starlight. Phosphorescent fungi glow faintly on the tree trunks.

exits.description: The trail opens north toward the village.
The trail descends deeper into the jungle to the south.
A game trail leads west into the wilds.

description: The jungle thickens and the trail narrows to a thread. Stone ruins peek through the vegetation — carved faces worn smooth by centuries of rain. The air is heavy and still.
dayDesc: The jungle at its densest. Stone faces stare from the undergrowth, half-consumed by roots. A carved stairway descends, each step shallower than the last.
nightDesc: The jungle at its densest. The stone faces are invisible but you can feel them watching. Fireflies drift between the ruins like slow sparks.

exits.description: The trail climbs north toward thinner jungle.
A stone path leads west through the ruins toward the sound of water.
A break in the canopy to the east reveals a sunlit glade.

description: A circular clearing where the jungle pulls back as if by agreement. Wildflowers carpet the ground. A flat stone in the center has been worn smooth by sitting, or kneeling.
dayDesc: A circular clearing full of wildflowers. The flat stone in the center is warm. Birdsong fills the space from every direction, unsourced.
nightDesc: A circular clearing. The flowers have closed. The flat stone in the center is cold. The silence here is different from the jungle's silence — it's listening.

exits.description: The jungle wall rises to the west.

description: A dry path crosses the marine lagoon to join the main body of Spirit Island to the east with the adjoining Twin Island to the northwest.
dayDesc: A dry path crosses the marine lagoon. The water on both sides is shallow and crystal clear. Small sharks cruise the flats, unhurried.
nightDesc: At high tide, the marine lagoon rises to ankle depth over the causeway stones. The crossing is possible but uneasy.

exits.description: A stone path leads east into the jungle ruins.
A faint path curves southeast toward a break in the canopy.
A narrow rocky isthmus connects to the smaller island to the northwest.

description: A spine of black volcanic rock barely above the waterline connects the twin islands. Waves crash on both sides, throwing salt spray. The footing is treacherous.
dayDesc: A spine of black volcanic rock. Crabs scuttle into crevices as you pass. The twin island rises ahead, its peak crowned with scrub.
nightDesc: A spine of black rock indistinguishable from the black water except by the shock of spray. Crossing in the dark is an act of faith.

exits.description: The causeway stretches southeast to the main island.
A pocket beach lies on the lee side of the twin island.

description: A crescent of white sand sheltered from the prevailing wind. The water here is absurdly calm. Driftwood has been arranged into something that might be a shelter or might be art.
dayDesc: A crescent of white sand. The water is bath-warm and turquoise. Someone has arranged driftwood into a structure — a shelter, a sculpture, an altar.
nightDesc: A crescent of white sand under stars. The driftwood structure casts strange shadows. The water laps with metronomic patience.

exits.description: The rocky isthmus leads south to the main island.

description: A straight path between the two ancient oceans to the seat of power.
dayDesc: A straight path between the two ancient oceans to the seat of power. The sun smiles above and an angel points to a door to the north.
nightDesc: A straight path between the two ancient oceans to the seat of power. The triangle of their outlines shines against the dark wilderness to the north.

exits.description: Something to the north is possible now.
A stone path leads east into the jungle ruins.
A faint path curves southeast toward a break in the canopy.
A narrow rocky isthmus connects to the smaller island to the northwest.

description: Something is wrong with the road, it's blocked. Two rebounds lead to a watery pediment and a spandrel respectively.

exits.description: An island is nearby.
As is a path that diminishes.

description: The structures and trees around are barely silhouettes, they're more like pure potential.
dayDesc: The structures and trees around are barely silhouettes, they're more like pure potential.
nightDesc: You're somewhere in the Paths. The wind is punishing and visibility is nil.

exits.description: To the clockwise, a threadier path with more spandrels but less wind.
To widdershins, the warmth of the sun, felt if not seen.

description: A dimly lit parking lot limned by spiral roads that shrink away to nil rather than terminate.
dayDesc: A dimly lit parking lot limned by spiral roads that shrink away to nil rather than terminate.
nightDesc: The surroundings are opaque by night, it could be anywhere. It isn't windy.

exits.description: To the widdershins, the path leads up to someplace chaotic.
There's something down there, but how to reach it?

description: The descending arm of a spiral into a vast, neutral space, like the inside of a hangar. Something is watching this spot, the impression is of a whale's vast eye. A small box is bolted to the wall of the factory.

exits.description: Somewhere up there is a path out, but how to get there?
A catwalk leads to a toylike, human-scale door.
